summary: AIAIVN is a 2024-founded Vietnamese AI company whose stated mission is to bring world-class GenAI to Vietnamese enterprise — but whose current product catalog is almost entirely tourism and public-service deployments. The gap between stated mission and shipped offering is the most strategically significant feature of the company and the clearest signal of where partnership leverage exists.

atoms.claim: AIAIVN's stated mission is to empower Vietnamese businesses with world-leading AI, combining global expertise with local understanding at competitive cost.
atoms.evidence: About page tagline and mission section — global technology, solutions for Vietnam.

atoms.claim: AIAIVN's vision is to be the leading force revolutionizing Vietnamese enterprise through cutting-edge AI technology.
atoms.evidence: Vision statement on about page — leading force in business revolution through AI.

atoms.claim: AIAIVN's mission statement describes enterprise-wide AI transformation, but their published solution catalog is exclusively tourism and public-service deployments.
atoms.evidence: Solutions page lists only tourism products; About page describes broad enterprise ambition.

atoms.claim: HeriStepAI is their location-triggered multilingual audio guide product, using GPS and beacon technology for heritage sites and museums.
atoms.evidence: Solutions page description of HeriStepAI with GPS beacon trigger and multilingual output.

atoms.claim: Travist is their real-time multilingual speech translation product aimed at tourism, hospitality, and sales conversations.
atoms.evidence: Solutions page shows Travist with live speech translation use cases across industries.

atoms.claim: Their AI Virtual Assistant offers 24/7 GenAI-powered customer support across 20+ languages, deployable on web, kiosk, robot, mobile, Messenger, and Zalo.
atoms.evidence: Solutions page lists AI Virtual Assistant with multi-channel deployment across 20+ languages.

atoms.claim: Robot AI is their service-robot offering for events, hotels, airports, conference centers, and exhibitions.
atoms.evidence: Solutions page describes Robot AI for events and public spaces as technology staff.

atoms.claim: Their multilingual self-service Ticketing Kiosk handles ticket purchase, payment, and invoice printing for tourism destinations and museums.
atoms.evidence: Solutions page covers Ticketing Kiosk with POS and invoice integration.

atoms.claim: Anthony Tuan Phan is Chairman and CEO, with 20+ years of distributed-systems engineering at Amazon, eBay, Techcombank, and Meta before founding AIAIVN.
atoms.evidence: LinkedIn and About page — CEO with engineering career across FAANG and Vietnamese banking.

atoms.claim: CEO Anthony Phan spent 2020-2021 as Director and Data Architect at Techcombank, leading their core data platform migration from on-prem to cloud data lake.
atoms.evidence: LinkedIn work history — Techcombank Director, Data Architect role, cloud migration focus.

atoms.claim: CEO Anthony Phan has applied security background — HP Security Lab research intern and Amazon Multipass credential distribution system engineer.
atoms.evidence: LinkedIn shows HP Security Lab internship and Amazon credentials infrastructure work.

atoms.claim: CEO Anthony Phan taught Internet Technology and Distributed Systems at Vietnam National University and Rutgers University before his industry career.
atoms.evidence: LinkedIn shows lecturer positions at VNU Ho Chi Minh City and Rutgers.

atoms.claim: Yasuno Ken is Co-founder and CTO with a Meta engineering background.
atoms.evidence: About page lists Ken as Co-founder/CTO with Meta credential in advisor bench description.

atoms.claim: My Huong is Co-founder and Chief Growth Officer, the likely primary owner of external partnership conversations.
atoms.evidence: About page lists My Huong as Co-founder/CGO in strategy and business development section.

atoms.claim: Their technical advisory bench draws from Microsoft, Meta, Google, Amazon, NVIDIA, and Stripe, including PhDs from Carnegie Mellon, UC, and Rutgers.
atoms.evidence: About page lists advisory team with named senior roles at FAANG and NVIDIA plus academic credentials.

atoms.claim: Their architecture is GenAI-first, combining LLM, speech-to-text, text-to-speech, real-time translation, and face recognition into deployable vertical products.
atoms.evidence: Solutions pages describe GenAI as the foundation across HeriStepAI, Travist, Virtual Assistant.

atoms.claim: They deliver products across web, kiosk hardware, robot platforms, mobile apps, Messenger, and Zalo — indicating substantial integration engineering capacity.
atoms.evidence: Virtual Assistant page lists all six delivery channels as supported deployment targets.

atoms.claim: Da Nang Museum is a live deployment running their automatic multilingual audio narration and AI robot greeter systems.
atoms.evidence: Projects page shows Da Nang Museum as a live deployment with multiple AIAIVN products.

atoms.claim: Hoi An ancient town has their automated ticketing kiosks deployed for tourist ticketing flows.
atoms.evidence: Projects page names Hoi An among live kiosk deployments.

atoms.claim: Their face-recognition check-in and crowd-management systems have been deployed at Vietnamese marathon events.
atoms.evidence: Projects page shows marathon deployments using face-recognition check-in flow.

atoms.claim: They display 18 partner logos on their about page, though the strategic depth of these relationships is not disclosed publicly.
atoms.evidence: About page partner section shows 18 partner logos without public detail on relationship depth.

atoms.claim: Stated customer verticals are museums, heritage sites, tourism operators, public admin centers, shopping centers, hotels, airports, and event venues.
atoms.evidence: Solutions pages list target customer types across tourism and public sectors.

atoms.claim: Mission statement implies a much broader ICP including Vietnamese banks, insurers, hospitals, manufacturers, telcos, and government ministries — even though none are listed in current products.
atoms.evidence: Mission text covers enterprise broadly while solution catalog covers only tourism subset.

atoms.claim: Their positioning emphasizes cost-competitive delivery against Western AI vendors, combining FAANG-caliber engineering with Vietnamese operating costs.
atoms.evidence: About page positions them as world-class technology delivered at Vietnamese cost structure.

atoms.claim: Vietnamese-first UX, deep cultural adaptation, and Vietnamese-language product surface are their strongest moat against Western AI entrants.
atoms.evidence: Website is Vietnamese-first with localized content, UX flows, and cultural framing.

atoms.claim: Ms. My Nhung is listed as Key Account Manager with email nhung.ha@aiaivn.com and phone 0844-591-010.
atoms.evidence: About page contact section lists Ms. My Nhung as Key Account Manager with direct contact info.

atoms.claim: General contact is info@aiaivn.com and 0856-008-010, with offices in Da Nang ICT Park 2 and HCMC Bay Hien Ward.
atoms.evidence: Footer contact block lists both offices and general inbound email and phone.

atoms.claim: Founded in 2024 with revenue concentrated in tourism pilots, they face structural pressure to expand horizontally to match mission scope and justify investor expectations.
atoms.evidence: 2024 founding date plus tourism-only catalog implies growth-stage horizontal expansion pressure.

atoms.claim: No visible outbound sales motion, channel program, or structured sales enablement — their go-to-market appears to rely on inbound, referral, and direct demos.
atoms.evidence: No evidence of outbound campaigns or channel programs on website or public content.

atoms.claim: Pricing is entirely quote-gated, which slows sales cycles and limits self-service expansion.
atoms.evidence: Pricing page routes to inquiry form rather than published pricing tiers.

atoms.claim: They operate from Da Nang ICT Park 2 and Ho Chi Minh City Bay Hien Ward — the two principal Vietnamese tech corridors.
atoms.evidence: Footer and contact pages confirm both Da Nang and HCMC office locations.
